Verify that the systemT is a basis. 1 b. A company manufactures two kinds of leather purses, A and B. A is a high-quality purse, and B is a lower quality. The sales of each of these purses, A and B, earn a profit of Rs.4 and Rs.3, respectively. Each purse of type A requires twice as much time as a purse of type B. If all purses are of type B, the company could make 1000 purses per day. The supply of leather is sufficient for only 800 purses per day . Purse A requires a fancy buckle, and only 400 buckles per day are available. There are only 700 buckles available for B. What should be the daily production of each type of purse for maximum profit?
